CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Continued To Dec-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Summary Judgment Of Defendants Regents Of The University Of California, Maripaule Thiet, M.D., And Melinda Lorenson, M.D.
Off Calendar Oct-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Genee Woodson (707.367.3135) appears telephonically as plaintiff/opposing party, In Propria Persona

Attorney, Erin R. Sabey of Donnelly Nelson DePolo & Murray (925.287.8181) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

Motion For Summary Judgment Of Defendants Regents Of The University Of California, Maripaule Thiet, M.D. And Melinda Lorenson, M.D. is GRANTED as of this date. After the last hearing, Ms. Woodson notified the court that she was contesting the tentative ruling. The tentative ruling is: The Regents Defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants have maintained their summary judgment burden of production by providing the declaration of the medical expert Dr. Byrne. That declaration shows that defendants did not breach the standard of care or cause legal injury to plaintiffs. In opposition, plaintiffs fail to present any competing expert testimony to create a triable issue of material fact. "When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence." (Munro v. UC Regents (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985.) Plaintiffs fail to show that expert testimony is unnecessary and the "common knowledge" exception applies. (See Flowers v. Torrance Memorial Hospital Medical Center (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001.) The written order granting summary judgment, filed September 8, 2016 is ordered vacated for purposes of re-entry as of this date. The prevailing party shall submit a duplicate [proposed] order, tracking verbatim the vacated order of September 8, 2016 for signature and re-entry effective as of October 14, 2016.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendants have maintained their summary judgment burden of production by providing the declaration of the medical expert Dr. Byrne. That declaration shows that defendants did not breach the standard of care or cause legal injury to plaintiffs. In opposition, plaintiffs fail to present any competing expert testimony to create a triable issue of material fact. "When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence." (Munro v. UC Regents (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985.) Plaintiffs fail to show that expert testimony is unnecessary and the "common knowledge" exception applies. (See Flowers v. Torrance Memorial Hospital Medical Center (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.
